info.name: Labor Project Backend - Local
info.description:
Local Postman collection for Labor Project Backend.
Every request shows its contract explicitly in that request: method, URL, required headers, query params, and body.
Protected requests use Header: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}.
Run Auth > POST /api/auth/login first to get access_token and refresh_token.

variable.description: Local backend base URL for testing.
Local backend WebSocket base URL for Worker Mobile realtime connection.
Bearer access token returned by POST /api/auth/login. Used in Authorization header.
Refresh token returned by POST /api/auth/login. Used by POST /api/auth/refresh body.
Token returned when login gets ACTIVE_SESSION_EXISTS. Used by confirm-force login.
Client device id sent in login body.
Client device name sent in login body.
Worker code or username used by Admin Workers /:id routes, for example MN000115.
Lower-rank admin account id used by /api/admin/users/{{adminUserId}}/permissions. Set by POST /api/admin/admins.
vehicle_job_ref returned by POST /api/gate/vehicle-jobs and WORKER_ASSIGNED.
stall_job_ref used by admin stall APIs and worker ticket completion.
product_ref sent by Gate and used by worker product quantity confirmation.
Signed token from the vendor LINE completion message: copy value after action=vendor_confirm_completion&token=.
Signed token from the vendor LINE completion message: copy value after action=vendor_reject_completion&token=.
Driver QR token returned by POST /api/gate/vehicle-jobs.
Worker QR token returned by POST /api/gate/vehicle-jobs or WORKER_ASSIGNED.
Driver guest session token returned by POST /api/driver/qr-sessions.
Worker code used by admin assignment APIs.

request.description:
Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/auth/login
Auth: No auth required
Headers: Content-Type: application/json
Query: None
Body: Admin username/password only.
Response: login returns tokens only: access_token, refresh_token, token_type, expires_in.
Notes: Saves access_token and refresh_token into collection variables when login succeeds. Call GET /api/auth/me after login to fetch admin profile data, permissions, and latest activity.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/auth/login
Auth: No auth required
Headers: Content-Type: application/json
Query: None
Body: Worker username/password plus device_id and device_name.
Response: login returns tokens only: access_token, refresh_token, token_type, expires_in.
Notes: Saves access_token and refresh_token into collection variables when login succeeds. If another worker session exists on a different device, saves login_challenge_token for POST /api/auth/login/confirm-force. Call GET /api/auth/me after login to fetch worker profile data. After successful worker login, open WS /ws/workers, then call POST /api/workers/me/online.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/auth/login/confirm-force
Auth: No auth required
Headers: Content-Type: application/json
Query: None
Body: JSON: login_challenge_token, device_id, device_name
Notes: Use only after login returns ACTIVE_SESSION_EXISTS with a login_challenge_token.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/auth/refresh
Auth: No auth required
Headers: Content-Type: application/json
Query: None
Body: JSON: refresh_token
Notes: Returns a new access_token and refresh_token.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <access_token>.

request.description:
Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/auth/logout
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Query: None
Body: None
Notes: Backend reads session_id from access token and revokes that session.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <access_token>.

request.description:
Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/auth/me
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Query: None
Body: None
Notes: Returns current account from access token. Admin response includes permission_level and permissions.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <access_token>.

formdata.description: Optional worker profile image. Allowed: jpg, png, webp. Max 5 MB.

request.description:
Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}. Do not set Content-Type manually for multipart/form-data; Postman sets the boundary.
Query: None
Body: multipart/form-data from create-worker UI. Optional file field: image. Text fields: full_name, phone, nationality, shirt_type, shirt_number, work_start_date, shift_start_time, shift_end_time, password, status.
Response: message only, Worker created successfully.
Notes: Admin only. username defaults to worker_code generated from shirt_number, such as MN000004. password defaults to phone if omitted. nationality_name defaults to nationality and nationality_code defaults to UNKNOWN when omitted. work_start_date is the first working date. Shift availability uses only shift_start_time and shift_end_time; work_date is no longer required. shirt_number is saved separately and also used to generate worker_code. Uploaded image is stored under /uploads/workers and saved as profile.image_url. JSON body with image_url/img is still supported when no file upload is needed.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <access_token>.

request.description:
Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users?page=1&limit=20&search=&status=
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Query: page, limit, search, status
Body: None
Notes: Admin only. Lists worker accounts newest first. Response item is trimmed for worker table UI only: worker_code, shirt_number, full_name, work_schedule, status, updated_at. Shift availability uses only shift_start_time and shift_end_time.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <access_token>.

request.description:
Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users/{{userId}}
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Query: None
Body: None
Notes: Admin only. {{userId}} must be worker_code or username, for example MN000115. Response is trimmed for worker edit UI: image_url, worker_code, full_name, status, and details.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <access_token>.

request.description:
Method: PATCH
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users/{{userId}}
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}, Content-Type: application/json
Query: None
Body: JSON fields from worker edit UI: image_url/img, worker_code, full_name, phone, status, position, shirt_number, shirt_type, work_start_date, shift_start_time, shift_end_time. work_date is optional legacy only. Nested profile/work_schedule is still supported for backward compatibility.
Notes: Admin only. {{userId}} must be worker_code or username. If shirt_number is sent without worker_code, backend regenerates worker_code/username as MN + 6 digits. status inactive revokes active worker sessions. Password reset remains separate at PATCH /api/admin/users/{{userId}}/password.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <access_token>.

request.description:
Method: PATCH
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users/{{userId}}/password
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}, Content-Type: application/json
Query: None
Body: JSON: new_password
Notes: Admin only. {{userId}} must be worker_code or username. Resets worker password and revokes worker sessions.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <access_token>.

request.description:
Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/jobs/workers/{{userId}}/work-schedules?page=1&limit=20
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Query: page, limit
Body: None
Notes: Admin Jobs. {{userId}} must be worker_code or username. Returns the current worker work schedule for job operations.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/jobs/workers/status
Auth: Admin access token
Body: None
Notes: Admin Jobs. Lists only active workers who are online and inside their current shift. Response is shaped for the worker queue board with full_name, worker_code, shirt_number, image_url, shift_name, latest_activity_at, and one status only. UI maps status to its own columns. Board status values: open_app, ready, assigned, working, break.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/jobs/workers/status/{{userId}}
Auth: Admin access token
Body: None
Notes: Admin Jobs. {{userId}} must be worker_code or username. Shows one worker queue status, active assignment, and heartbeat presence.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/jobs/workers/{{userId}}/status/force
Auth: Admin access token
Body: status ready|waiting|offline|break, optional reason
Notes: Admin Jobs. {{userId}} must be worker_code or username. ready puts worker into Redis queue, waiting removes worker from dispatch queue, break starts a 15-minute break and consumes a break count, offline removes worker from queue.

query.description: Optional. If omitted, all dates are returned.
Optional. If omitted, all matching rows are returned.
Optional page size used only when page is sent.

request.description:
Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/vehicle-jobs?date=2026-07-07&page=1&limit=20&search=&status=
Auth: Admin access token
Query: date/search/status optional. page optional; when page is omitted backend returns all matching rows ordered by latest created_at. When page is sent backend returns that page and pagination metadata.
Response: trimmed list rows only: vehicle_job_ref, gate_transaction_ref, license_plate, vehicle_type, workers_required, status. QR tokens are available from the detail/QR flows, not this list.
Notes: Search can match vehicle, market, stall, ticket, or license plate references.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/vehicle-jobs/{{vehicleJobRef}}
Auth: Admin access token
Notes: Returns vehicle job detail with markets, stall jobs, and products. Saves first marketJobRef, stallJobRef, and productRef when available.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/vehicle-jobs/{{vehicleJobRef}}/cancel
Auth: Admin access token
Response: message, vehicle_job_ref, status.
Notes: Cancels the entire vehicle job, all markets, all stall jobs, and active assignments. Workers from active assignments are moved to waiting.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/vehicle-jobs/{{vehicleJobRef}}/cancel-and-requeue
Auth: Admin access token
Body: optional reason
Response: message, vehicle_job_ref, status, requeued_worker_codes.
Notes: Cancels the whole vehicle job and returns affected workers to the tail of the Redis queue. If multiple workers are affected, backend randomly shuffles that group before enqueueing.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/vehicle-jobs/{{vehicleJobRef}}/assign-workers
Auth: Admin access token
Body: worker_codes array
Notes: Admin manually assigns the vehicle job to selected workers. Workers must be active and must not already have an active assignment. Backend marks them busy and starts the 1-minute accept timeout.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/vehicle-jobs/{{vehicleJobRef}}/scan-deadline/extend
Auth: Admin access token
Body: minutes required. Omit worker_codes to extend all ACCEPTED workers in the vehicle job; send worker_codes to extend selected workers only.
Example selected worker body: { "minutes": 10, "worker_codes": ["MN000115"] }
Response: vehicle_job_ref, worker_qr_token, assignments[].worker_code/status/scan_deadline_at.
Notes: Extends scan_deadline_at only. worker_qr_token stays the same and is reused for check-in after extension.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/vehicle-jobs/{{vehicleJobRef}}/workers/{{workerCode}}/assignment/cancel
Auth: Admin access token
Body: optional reason
Notes: Cancels one active worker assignment by vehicle_job_ref + worker_code and moves that worker to waiting. Backend does not auto-fill a replacement worker.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/market-jobs/{{marketJobRef}}/cancel
Auth: Admin access token
Notes: Cancels one market job and all stall jobs under that market.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/stall-jobs/{{stallJobRef}}/cancel
Auth: Admin access token
Notes: Cancels one stall job only.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/stall-jobs/{{stallJobRef}}/reopen
Auth: Admin access token with jobs:reopen permission
Body: None
Notes: Reopens one closed stall job so workers can submit corrected quantities again.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/settings
Auth: Admin access token with settings:read permission
Body: None
Notes: Returns runtime settings used by backend calculations.

Method: PATCH
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/settings
Auth: Admin access token with settings:update permission
Body: Partial JSON is allowed. Updated settings affect new calculations after this request; existing assignment deadlines already saved in DB remain unchanged.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/roles
Auth: Admin access token with roles:read permission
Body: None
Notes: Lists admin permission levels ordered from highest to lowest. Permissions are stored per admin account in DB, not derived from this response.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/admins
Auth: Admin access token with admins:create permission
Body: username, password, full_name, optional position/status, permission_level owner|manager|supervisor, and permissions array
Notes: Creates role=admin only. Seed owner admin can create manager/supervisor. Manager can create supervisor only. No API can create owner because the actor must have a higher permission_level than the new account. Saves created account id to {{adminUserId}}.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users/{{adminUserId}}/permissions
Auth: Admin access token with permissions:read permission
Body: None
Notes: Use an admin account id, not a worker id.

Method: PATCH
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users/{{adminUserId}}/permissions
Auth: Admin access token with permissions:update permission
Body: permission_level and permissions array
Notes: Updates admin permissions and revokes active sessions of that admin account. Permission is stored per account from DB. The actor must have a higher permission_level rank than both the target admin and the new permission_level. The target admin must login again to receive the latest permissions in the token.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/gate/vehicle-jobs
Auth: No auth required in mock phase
Headers: Content-Type: application/json
Body: Gate mock payload. gate_transaction_ref is idempotency key, vehicle_job_ref identifies the vehicle job, market_job_ref identifies each market job, stall_job_ref identifies each stall job, product_ref identifies each product, and ticket_no is an optional external bill number for driver/admin lookup. Set dispatch_now true when the vehicle is ready to unload immediately; omit or false to keep the Driver ready flow.
Response: trimmed acknowledgement only: result, message, vehicle_job.vehicle_job_ref/gate_transaction_ref/license_plate/status, and qr driver/worker tokens.
Result: 201 + result CREATED for a new job, 200 + result REPLAYED when gate_transaction_ref is retried with the same payload, 409 when vehicle_job_ref already exists or gate_transaction_ref is reused with a different payload.
Notes: Saves gateResult, vehicleJobRef, driverQrToken, and workerQrToken collection variables. Market/stall/product refs come from the request body or Admin detail endpoint. With dispatch_now true, backend creates the job as DISPATCH_NOW and immediately dispatches ready workers through WebSocket.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/driver/qr-sessions
Auth: No auth required
Body: JSON qr_token from driver QR
Notes: Creates a 24-hour driver guest session and saves driverSessionToken.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/driver/jobs/current
Auth: Driver guest session via Authorization: Bearer {{driverSessionToken}}
Body: None

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/driver/jobs/{{vehicleJobRef}}/ready
Auth: Driver guest session
Body: None
Response: vehicle_job_ref, license_plate, status, worker_qr_token.
Notes: Marks vehicle job DISPATCH_NOW and dispatches ready workers from Redis FIFO queue.

item.name: Worker Application
item.description: WebSocket setup: Postman HTTP request items cannot open ws:// as a native WebSocket tab. Create it manually with New > WebSocket, then use {{wsBaseUrl}}/ws/workers?token={{accessToken}} and click Connect. Do this after POST /api/auth/login - Worker and before POST /api/workers/me/online. WORKER_CONNECTED payload returns worker_code, not internal account_id. The socket only confirms the worker realtime connection is alive; the worker becomes online/ready after POST /api/workers/me/online.

request.description:
Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/online
Auth: Worker access token
Body: None
Response: full_name, worker_code, status, today_job_count, break_count_used, completed_job_count.
Notes: Puts worker into Redis FIFO queue only during the assigned shift time. Returns 403 if the worker has no current schedule or is outside the assigned shift. OUTSIDE_WORK_SHIFT includes shift and remaining_time text for the mobile modal.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/offline
Auth: Worker access token
Body: None
Response: full_name, worker_code, status, today_job_count, break_count_used, completed_job_count. status is offline after the worker is removed from Redis queue.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/break
Auth: Worker access token
Body: None
Response: full_name, worker_code, status, break_count_used, break_count_limit. status is break.
Notes: Puts worker into break status for 15 minutes. Requires worker to be ready and inside the current shift time. OUTSIDE_WORK_SHIFT includes shift and remaining_time text for the mobile modal. Max 5 breaks per shift. BullMQ returns worker to the tail of Redis queue automatically.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/status
Auth: Worker access token
Body: None
Response: full_name, worker_code, image_url, status, nationality, work_start_date, phone, and shift name/start/end. status comes from current worker queue state; if no queue state exists it returns offline. When status is break, response also includes break_until and remaining_break_time with total_seconds, minutes, seconds, and text.

query.description: Assignment created date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

request.description:
Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/assignments/history?date=2026-07-07
Auth: Worker access token
Query: date required, YYYY-MM-DD
Body: None
Response: date and data[].vehicle_job_ref/gate_transaction_ref/license_plate/status/accepted_at/completed_at/created_at.
Notes: Returns assignment history for the current worker only. The date is interpreted in Asia/Bangkok time and filters by assignment created_at.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/assignments/{{vehicleJobRef}}/accept
Auth: Worker access token
Body: None
Path: {{vehicleJobRef}} comes from WORKER_ASSIGNED.vehicle_job_ref.
Notes: Assignment must be PENDING and accepted within 1 minute. Response is trimmed for the worker job detail screen only: license_plate, team[].full_name/worker_code/image_url/scan_status, markets[].market_name/stall_count/stalls[].stall_job_ref/stall_code/stall_name/product_count/products[].product_ref/name/quantity/unit. Use stalls[].stall_job_ref later with POST /api/workers/me/tickets/{{stallJobRef}}/complete.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/assignments/{{vehicleJobRef}}/check-in-qr
Auth: Worker access token
Path: {{vehicleJobRef}} comes from WORKER_ASSIGNED.vehicle_job_ref.
Body: JSON qr_token from worker QR
Response: status, worker_code, vehicle_job_ref, worker_qr_token. Normally { "status": "SCANNED", "worker_code": "MN000142", "vehicle_job_ref": "VEH-20260706-0001", "worker_qr_token": "worker_qr_xxx" }.
Notes: Checks worker into the job only while scan_deadline_at is still valid. If expired, returns QR_EXPIRED. Admin can extend scan_deadline_at and the worker can retry using the same worker_qr_token. The returned worker_qr_token is the same QR value that was scanned and can be rendered by the mobile app so other assigned workers can scan from this worker screen. If checked-in workers reach workers_required, vehicle job moves to IN_PROGRESS.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/tickets/{{stallJobRef}}/complete
Auth: Worker access token
Body: items required. Send every product_ref in the ticket with confirmed_quantity counted by the worker.
Notes: {{stallJobRef}} is the stall_job_ref from Gate payload/response, such as STALLJOB-001. Any worker mapped to this ticket can submit, but only the first request while the ticket is IN_PROGRESS or COMPLETION_REJECTED succeeds. The ticket becomes WAITING_VENDOR_CONFIRM and vendor is notified via LINE queue with signed confirm/reject tokens. When LINE_DEBUG_POSTBACK_RESPONSE=true, response includes debug_line_postback and this request saves vendorConfirmToken/vendorRejectToken automatically.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/events
Auth: Admin access token
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}, Accept: text/event-stream
Body: None
Notes: This is an Admin-only SSE stream, not a JSON list. It stays open and receives realtime dashboard events. Snapshot/latest state should still be loaded from Admin REST APIs.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/line/webhook
Auth: No auth. LINE signature is required only when LINE_CHANNEL_SECRET is configured.
Body: Mock LINE postback. userId must match vendor_line_id. Copy vendorConfirmToken from the LINE completion message token= value. Confirms the waiting ticket completion and notifies worker/admin. If this confirmation makes every ticket in the vehicle job terminal, backend closes the market/vehicle job and completes active worker assignments so workers can go online for the next job.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/line/webhook
Auth: No auth. LINE signature is required only when LINE_CHANNEL_SECRET is configured.
Body: Mock LINE postback. userId must match vendor_line_id. Copy vendorRejectToken from the LINE completion message token= value. Rejects the waiting ticket completion, then the worker can submit the ticket completion again.
